title: parahebe raoulii
intro: Parahebe raoulii, also known as Raoule's copper flower or Raoule's copper bells, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits associated with Parahebe raoulii include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant can be made into infusions, tinctures, or ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
